purpose: Current production bundle debt ceiling. The v3.51.23 export permission and client-integrity boundary measures 4,573,782 raw / 1,312,186 gzip JavaScript bytes on the reviewed source tree. Against the v3.51.22 clean artifact (4,570,199 / 1,310,246), this is +3,583 raw / +1,940 gzip (under 0.15%) for exact reports/audit/export permission-entitlement isolation, typed fail-closed legacy export reads, stable transport/artifact/download error codes, stale-scope and single-flight guards, pre-download audit enforcement, overlap-correct leave selection, truthful XLS naming, XML 1.0 character filtering, XML/HTML escaping and shared formula-neutralized CSV generation. No database, Edge wire contract, CSS or dependency was added. The existing ceilings leave only 19-byte raw and 143-byte gzip total allowances; largest raw and gzip leave 128-byte and 29-byte allowances. Further increases require an explicit reviewed baseline update.
